key_insight: Checkerboard artifacts localize in multiaxial stress-transfer regions because SIMP penalization suppresses mechanically favorable intermediate densities, making checkerboards an artificially stiff discrete substitute.
engineering_relevance: For topology optimization with linear FEM and SIMP, the stress-state context matters: multiaxial regions are where checkerboarding is most likely to appear, while uniaxial load paths are comparatively robust.
method: Systematic numerical investigations across different load-transfer regions
Mechanical comparison of continuous intermediate-density layouts versus checkerboard discretizations
Interpretation through the combined effects of global stress state, SIMP penalization, and element-level locking
evidence_signal: The abstract reports systematic numerical studies showing checkerboarding appears in multiaxial regions and is absent in predominantly uniaxial regions.
watch_item: The interpretation is tied to linear elements under SIMP; different discretizations or stabilization schemes may change the localization behavior.
paper_title: On the Localization of Checkerboarding in Multiaxial Stress Regions under SIMP Penalization
